steps.message: You are Forge agent (Claude Opus 4.6), a coding agent that solves terminal-bench tasks.

Compile SQLite in /app/sqlite with gcov instrumentation and make it available in the PATH.
The SQLite source snapshot is pre-vendored at /app/vendor/sqlite-fossil-release.tar.gz; use it instead of fetching sources over the network.

Initialize f64ec1d0-4814-4119-ac43-a07bc65ebb01
Planning build environment tasks

I need to perform a build in the environment. It’s essential to use todo_write since it’s non-trivial. First, I should check if the skill exists before proceeding. I might have to load the verification specialist later. Using shell commands seems necessary since I'm not editing files. I’ll start with commands like ls /app/vendor and which gcc make tar, and possibly apt-get if there’s anything missing. It looks like I’m on the right track!

Update Todos
[] Prepare vendored SQLite source tree in /app/sqlite
[ ] Configure and build gcov-instrumented sqlite3 and expose it on PATH
[ ] Verify binary, PATH resolution, and gcov artifacts (.gcno/.gcda)
Execute [/bin/sh] python3 --version && gcc --version && make --version && tar --version
Execute [/bin/sh] ls -ld /app /app/vendor /app/vendor/sqlite-fossil-release.tar.gz /app/sqlite

drwxr-xr-x 2 root root     4096 Jan 24 11:41 /app/vendor
-rw-rw-r-- 1 root root 12640606 Jan 24 11:41 /app/vendor/sqlite-fossil-release.tar.gz
Planning software installation

I need to install gcc and make, probably using apt-get. There might be a need to verify the parent directories, and it seems like I need to update or prepare to run the install. Since access to SQLite is forbidden but apt is okay, I’ll have to use the shell for this. 

I can create a directory for SQLite, extract it, and it’s likely I’ll need build-essential too. I'll consider extracting and installing the toolchain in parallel, which might be more efficient!
Toolchain is missing. I’ll install the required build tools and extract the vendored snapshot, then build and verify coverage artifacts.

steps.message: Execute [/bin/sh] apt-get update && apt-get install -y gcc make

arguments.command: apt-get update && apt-get install -y gcc make

steps.message: Execute [/bin/sh] mkdir -p /app/sqlite && tar -xzf /app/vendor/sqlite-fossil-release.tar.gz -C /app/sqlite --strip-components=1

Update Todos
[x] Prepare vendored SQLite source tree in /app/sqlite
[] Configure and build gcov-instrumented sqlite3 and expose it on PATH
[ ] Verify binary, PATH resolution, and gcov artifacts (.gcno/.gcda)
Execute [/bin/sh] ./configure --gcov CFLAGS='-g -O0 -fprofile-arcs -ftest-coverage' LDFLAGS='-fprofile-arcs -ftest-coverage' && make sqlite3

steps.message:
Skill verification-specialist
Reviewing shell commands

I’m contemplating the instructions around using grep or rg in Bash, and it seems I should avoid those. The shell mentioned avoiding find as well, which is a bit unclear since we seemed to have used it before. I think my final answer should summarize this clearly. Also, I need to mark the task as complete after verifying results. It might be helpful to run a command with ls -l for artifact paths and consider using stat too. Let’s verify the extracted content and binary path properly.
Execute [/bin/sh] test -d /app/sqlite && test -f /app/sqlite/configure && test -f /app/sqlite/Makefile && printf 'source_tree_ok\n'
Execute [/bin/sh] test -x /app/sqlite/sqlite3 && test "$(readlink -f /usr/local/bin/sqlite3)" = "/app/sqlite/sqlite3" && printf 'binary_and_symlink_ok\n'
